text: Harness installed on cloud branch; commit harness/memory/memory.jsonl before session end or lessons are lost.

Removed 2 uncited hardware refs (Loihi/SpiNNaker) for IEEE compliance and scope; signposted existing Limitations paragraph; trimmed keywords 8->6. Citation integrity clean (23/23). papercheck submission: 2 errors -> 1 (only abstract length 274>200 remains).

Added Ethics statement: secondary analysis of de-identified data collected by Stony Brook Univ. Lab for Clinical Affective Neuroscience under its IRB; authors did no new collection. Mirrors DEAP handling. Clears papercheck ethics.statement flag. No IRB number fabricated.

CFP confirmed: IEEE BIBM 2026 Doctoral Forum is SINGLE-blind (author names required, no anonymized twin needed); 6-page limit EXCLUDING references and appendices; 1-page first-author CV appendix required. Set papercheck venue.blinding=single. Committed PDF: main=7pp incl refs, with_cv=8pp. CV appendix is real (A. Lane).
Tightened abstract 274->253 words, all 10 numeric tokens preserved. Remaining abstract.length flag is the tool's 200-word default; BIBM CFP sets no abstract limit, so it is non-blocking cosmetic.

No with-CV source existed; main_bibm2026_with_cv.pdf was a manual PDF merge of paper+CV (different doc classes). Committed PDFs are stale after source edits. Added main_bibm2026_with_cv.tex (pdfpages wrapper) and BUILD.md so the combined submission PDF is reproducible: build paper, build CV, then build wrapper.

Built submission PDFs from current source with pdflatex (installed via HTTPS apt mirrors). Clean compile: 0 undefined refs/citations, 0 overfull boxes. Paper body ends p6 (Ethics/Data Avail on p6), references tail to p7 -> body within 6-page limit (refs excluded). main_bibm2026_with_cv.pdf = 8pp (6 body + 1 ref + 1 CV), contains ethics statement + CV. This is the submission file.

Per author preference: replaced 'audit' (5 prose uses) with 'characterize/characterization' (matches conference title theme; left figure filename fig_pipeline_bsc6_audit.png). Removed all 30 em-dashes (---), replaced with parentheses/commas/colons per grammar; left en-dashes (numeric ranges, structure--function). Recompiled clean: 0 undefined, 0 overfull, body within 6pp.

Author revised Ethics statement to name the SHAPE study and drop the secondary-analysis/de-identified qualifiers: 'The SHAPE study was collected by the Stony Brook University Laboratory for Clinical Affective Neuroscience under that laboratory's IRB oversight. DEAP is public [koelstra2012deap].' Kept the \textbf{Ethics.} label; papercheck ethics check still PASS; body within 6pp.

Data Availability revised to point to SHAPE dataset contact (Laboratory for Clinical Affective Neuroscience, Stony Brook, lab-can.com) + DEAP public. Dropped prior 'access-controlled/aggregate-only' and 'clinical labels not diagnostic' sentences per author. Clean compile; body within 6pp.

Named the primary dataset throughout: 'Stress, Health, and the Psychophysiology of Emotion (SHAPE) project' at first mention in methods with lab pointer (Laboratory for Clinical Affective Neuroscience, Stony Brook, lab-can.com); 'our cohort'/'primary cohort' -> SHAPE in fig caption, DEAP section, limitations. Author confirmed the 'clinical labels not diagnostic' disclaimer is unnecessary (engineering paper) - kept removed. Clean compile, body within 6pp.

Converted SHAPE lab pointer from inline \url in body to a proper numbered reference [24] (\bibitem{shape}: Laboratory for Clinical Affective Neuroscience, SHAPE project, Stony Brook; URL in the reference only, like DEAP). Cited in methods + Data Availability. Citation integrity 24/24 clean.

Final human-voice pass: varied intro verb (characterize->compare) to avoid echoing the abstract, replaced stilted 'namely'+colon in intro, and merged a clunky double-parenthesis in contribution 2. Numbers preserved, clean compile, body within 6pp. Bundle = main_bibm2026_with_cv.pdf (8pp).

Adopted author's humanized revision as main_bibm2026.tex. Verified against prior version: identical numeric value-set (no value changed/added/removed; only benign count shifts from abstract simplification), 0 em-dashes, 0 forbidden claims (only 'Perturbation Diagnostics' title and Maass 'Real-time computing' ref title), clean compile 7pp (body 6pp, refs p7), 24/24 citations. Reads more human: shorter sentences, measured tone, 'reservoir not a proposed winner'; ranking described as 'changes' (more accurate than 'reverses'). Rebuilt bundle 8pp.
